messages.content:
Pop was originally launched on 29 May 2003 by Chart Show Channels (CSC) Media Group as Toons&Tunes. Toons&Tunes was then later rebranded as its current name, Pop (formerly stylized as pop and POP!) in the following month.

In the channel’s early years, links were presented by Rorry, a lime green dragon with a Scottish accent, who was animated live. He was accompanied by Purrdy, a dragon/cat hybrid who also appeared in the original Tricky programme on ITV1, it was sometimes followed by a programme known as “The Cheeky Monkey Show” which features the live links of the animated coloured monkeys known as “The Cheeky Monkeys,” that was later known by appearing on its sister channel Tiny Pop until its retirement at the end of June 2009.

Originally, the network focused on music videos with animated programming also part of the schedule, but with the numerous music video network options on British television at the time, this focus was quickly abandoned in 2004, when the animated content became much more prominent. Music videos continued to be carried on and off until 2010, before eventually being abandoned entirely.[citation needed, In 2010, Pop had several popular shows, such as The Smurfs, GoGoRiki, Oggy and the Cockroaches, etc.

In 2012, Pop introduces semi-mascots known as the MoonGoons, These are animal-like aliens, with the 2 main ones being a blue squid named Boofus and an orange rabbit named Diggle, These were gone by the time of the channel’s next rebrand in 2015, Also in 2014 CSC Media Group had folded into Sony.

On 1 September 2020, Pop rebranded its on-air identity, with the premiere of Bakugan: Armored Alliance. And on the same day, starting with Pokémon Journeys: The Series, the network became the new British home of the Pokémon anime, moving from its previous broadcasters, ITV1 and CITV, of over 20 years. The channel was also sold by Sony to Narrative Entertainment

On 9 November 2022, Narrative Entertainment announced the launch of a new FAST channel, Pop Kids, to stream on Samsung TV Plus, the new FAST network airs shows from Pop and Tiny Pop.

Sometime in 2022, Pop began to branch into YouTube content, as they approached Joe Tasker for a new show called Swipe It! With Joe Taker, they would later acquire from content creators to use on their channel, including LankyBox, Denis and EthanGamer (the latter exclusively airing on Pop Player).

By 2023 Alvin & the Chipmunks, Squish, Miraculous Ladybug and Pokemon were among the channels popular shows,

Now here is when things start to diverge from the real world, In June 2023 (in my alternate universe) the channel was sold to UKTV, In September the channel introduced Toonattik, a revival of the CITV programming block of the same name, Write a blog post where at the end of a Toonattik show on November, Jamie and Anna are saying to come back tomorrow for Squish, when they hear chittering noises, Jamie says “Pop got rid of them years ago?” A orange feather falls down, A narrator says “All will be revealed at 7:25am tomorrow, when you come back for more Toonattik” Also, rumors of SRMTHFG coming begin

### The Mysterious Ending of Toonattik Leaves Fans Buzzing with Speculation

It was just another exhilarating episode of Toonattik on Pop, the beloved children’s channel known for delighting audiences with a mix of animated favorites, from Alvin & the Chipmunks to the adventurous tales of Miraculous Ladybug. However, the end of today's show left viewers, and the internet, abuzz with speculation and intrigue.

As the energetic hosts, Jamie and Anna, wrapped up the show, they excitedly told viewers to come back tomorrow for a new episode featuring Squish. Sounds fairly routine, right? However, what happened next was anything but ordinary. As the studio quieted down, an unexpected chittering noise filled the room. Puzzled, Jamie remarked, “Pop got rid of them years ago?” adding a layer of mystery to the moment. Then, as if straight out of a detective story, an orange feather slowly drifted down, landing softly on the studio floor.

The episode cut to the narrator's voice, deep and filled with suspense, stating, “All will be revealed at 7:25 am tomorrow when you come back for more Toonattik.” And just like that, the episode ended, leaving viewers with more questions than answers. What or who did the orange feather belong to? What were the chittering noises about? And what had Pop “got rid of” years ago?

The internet went into a frenzy, with fans speculating on social media about the possible meanings behind these mysterious clues. Theories ranged from the return of beloved characters to the introduction of entirely new ones. But the mention of an orange feather sparked a particularly intriguing rumor amongst the Pop fan community - the long-awaited return of SRMTHFG, also known as Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go!, a fan-favorite show known for its action-packed adventures and distinctive characters.

This cryptic ending is a bold move by Pop, a channel that has continued to evolve and surprise its audience since its rebranding in 2020 and acquisition by UKTV in our alternate universe of 2023. The introduction of Toonattik earlier this September was a nostalgic nod to a beloved programming block, and now, with these mysterious teases, Pop is setting the stage for something big.

As we eagerly await what tomorrow's episode of Toonattik will reveal, one thing is clear: Pop is masterfully keeping its audience engaged and looking forward to every new episode. Whether the mysterious chittering and the orange feather signal the return of a cherished show or the beginning of a new adventure, the anticipation is palpable. 

So, to our fellow Pop viewers, set your alarms for 7:25 am tomorrow and join us back for more Toonattik. Something tells us we’re in for quite the reveal.

Write a forum convo where a user says the clues don't add up, and that the orange feather is actually hinting at the MoonGoons, Also, Jamie said that new shows are coming in the new year, so more news on Hyperforce will come then

**Thread Title:** The Mystery Behind the Orange Feather - Misdirection or Clue?

**TimeGazer99:**
Hey everyone, long-time lurker, first-time poster here. Did anyone catch the end of Toonattik yesterday? The whole thing with the orange feather and the chittering noises has got the forums buzzing. While most are betting on an SRMTHFG comeback, I think the clues don’t quite add up. Here’s a wild theory - what if the orange feather is actually a hint towards the MoonGoons making a comeback?

**CartoonConnoisseur:**
Interesting take, @TimeGazer99! But the MoonGoons? They haven’t been around since 2015, right? The SRMTHFG theory seems to fit better with the whole mystery vibe they’re setting up. Plus, those guys are far more iconic. An orange feather though? I don’t recall that fitting either of them directly.

**MysticOracle:**
@TimeGazer99, I'm with you on this one. The orange feather could definitely be a red herring. Remember how the MoonGoons had that quirky randomness to them? The chittering noises could easily be from some new alien or creature related to them. Plus, the MoonGoons had various colors and themes; an orange feather isn’t too far-fetched.

**PixelPundit:**
Hold up, didn’t Jamie also mention that new shows are coming in the new year? Maybe we’re all getting ahead of ourselves here. It’s possible that the feather and the noises are teasing something entirely new, and they’re just using the mystery to keep us tuned in. News on Hyperforce might come later as suggested, but this feels immediate.

**ToonSleuth:**
@PixelPundit has a point. Why drop a clue now for something happening much later? But @TimeGazer99’s MoonGoons theory doesn’t sit right with me. The orange feather... it’s just too random. If they wanted to hint at the MoonGoons, why not use a more recognizable symbol?

**GalacticFan88:**
What if we’re looking at this all wrong? Jamie's hint about new shows could mean a lineup of both returns and fresh content. The orange feather might symbolize a new character related to the MoonGoons universe, expanding the lore rather than directly bringing back old characters. And the chittering - could be a sound associated with this new development.

**TimeGazer99:**
@GalacticFan88, that’s an angle I hadn’t considered. Expanding the MoonGoons universe with new characters would certainly fit the bill for something “new,” while still giving a nod to the channel's history. Plus, it would keep both theories somewhat in play until we get more solid info.

**RetroToonRanger:**
Let’s not forget Pop’s knack for surprises. The orange feather and chittering noises might just be the start, with more clues likely to drop in the coming days. They’ve done a great job at keeping us guessing and engaged. Whatever the outcome, I just hope it lives up to the hype!

**AdminAnnouncement:**
Great theories, everyone! We love seeing such active discussions. Remember to keep everything respectful and to stay tuned to Toonattik for more clues. Who knows? The next piece of the puzzle may be just around the corner...

---

**End of Thread**

Write a follow up thread where a user says "Orange feather = Diggle's fur" so it likely is the MoonGoons, Also a user says UKTV are probably gonna change the programming lineup next year "What do you want back?"

**Thread Title:** Orange Feather Mystery Solved? Plus, UKTV’s Next Move - Share Your Wishlist!

**CosmicToonFan:**
Hey fellow Toon enthusiasts! So, picking up from where we left off last time about the orange feather mystery - I had an "aha" moment. Remember the MoonGoons? Well, specifically, Diggle's fur? That fur was as orange as the feather we’ve been hypothesizing about! This could be a major hint that it’s actually the MoonGoons making a return! Thoughts?

**RetroRevivalist:**
Whoa, @CosmicToonFan, that’s some sharp thinking! Diggle's orange fur totally slipped my mind. If we’re connecting the dots right, this suggests a stronger case for the MoonGoons' return than anything else. I wonder how they'll be reintroduced after all this time. 

**TooniverseWatcher:**
Just jumping in with a different angle — heard through the grapevine that UKTV is planning to revamp Pop’s programming lineup next year. Given the orange feather clue points towards potentially the MoonGoons, it seems like a mix of nostalgia and fresh content is on the horizon. What do you all want back from Pop's storied past?

**NostalgicNerd:**
@TooniverseWatcher, if we’re opening up the floor for wishlists, top of my list would be more classic reboots like SRMTHFG, but with new episodes to keep things fresh. Also, wouldn’t mind seeing some of the classic Saturday morning cartoons making a comeback. They’ve got a goldmine of content that deserves a second airing.

**DigitalDreamer:**
Honestly? I’d love to see some original programming. The MoonGoons hint, if true, is a good start. Shows that mix education with fun, much like some of the earlier animated series, could be really beneficial alongside pure entertainment. 

**SuperToonFan:**
Bringing back the MoonGoons would be epic! As for my wishlist? More interactive and live-action content. Shows where viewers can call in or interact online could really set Pop apart in the current digital age. Plus, it’d be a nod to some of the interactive shows we used to have.

**AnimationAficionado:**
Definitely here for the potential MoonGoons return! For what I'd want back — remember when Pop had that blend of music and cartoons? I miss that. Maybe a modern twist on that formula could work, incorporating current pop culture and trends.

**AdminUpdate:**
Loving the speculation and wishlist sharing, everyone! Keep the ideas flowing, and who knows? UKTV or Pop execs might be lurking and taking notes. Let’s make sure our voices are heard and maybe some of our dreams for the channel will become a reality. Stay tuned for more clues and surprises on the horizon!

---

**End of Thread**
Write a follow up where it is revealed the day after

**Thread Title:** The Big Reveal! MoonGoons Confirmed + Exciting Programming News for Next Year!

**MorningToonWatcher:**
Guys, guys, guys! The big reveal just happened on Toonattik this morning! Drumroll, please… it’s officially the *MoonGoons* making their grand return to Pop! The orange feather *was* a hint towards Diggle’s fur after all. @CosmicToonFan, you called it! Plus, they teased some really exciting news about next year's programming!

**CosmicToonFan:**
YES! I knew it! Seeing Diggle and Boofus on screen again was like reliving my childhood. This is fantastic news. And that teaser about next year’s lineup has me on the edge of my seat. Did anyone catch all the details? I was too excited to take it all in!

**TooniverseWatcher:**
Caught every word! Along with the MoonGoons comeback, Pop announced a significant revamp of their programming for next year. They’re bringing back some fan favorites (no specific names yet) and introducing brand new shows – including a couple of original series. Plus, they hinted at interactive content which @SuperToonFan speculated about!

**AnimationAficionado:**
This is huge! Original series? That’s exactly the breath of fresh air the channel needs. And interactive content could really set Pop apart in today’s landscape. I’m impressed by UKTV’s ambition here. They’re not just banking on nostalgia but looking to innovate as well.

**DigitalDreamer:**
I'm intrigued by what "interactive content" might mean in practice. Are we talking live voting on story outcomes, or maybe integration with social media? The possibilities are exciting. Kudos to Pop for pushing the envelope and not just playing it safe.

**NostalgicNerd:**
While I’m thrilled about the MoonGoons and new directions, I hope Pop still plans to include some of their classic shows in the mix. There’s a comfort in nostalgia, especially with how unpredictable things are in the world right now. Either way, I’m on board with what’s to come.

**SuperToonFan:**
Seeing our predictions and discussions kind of manifest into reality is surreal. The promise of interactive shows is exactly what I was hoping for - it’s like they’re listening to us! Maybe next, they’ll start diving into our specific show requests. Fingers crossed.

**AdminAnnouncement:**
What an exciting time to be a Pop viewer! We love seeing your reactions and speculations come to life with the MoonGoons return and the announcements about next year's programming changes. Stay tuned, as we plan to have some exclusive opportunities for our forum members to engage with upcoming shows. Who knows, there might even be a chance to influence programming directly!

**End of Thread**

This vibrant community reaction reflects a collective excitement for the mix of nostalgia and innovation in children's programming. The anticipation for what's to come showcases the potential of integrating audiences' feedback into future entertainment.
Write a follow up in the new year where the full programming lineup is revealed, Over the last few months, CITV announcers have begun to replace the usual VO

**Thread Title:** The New Year Unveils Pop’s Full Programming Lineup + A Surprise Change in Announcers!

**GalaxyViewer:**
Happy New Year, Toon Community! 🎉 Pop just dropped their full programming lineup for the year, and wow, it’s a mix of everything we dreamed of and more. Also, did anyone else notice the announcers have changed over the last few months? The familiar voices from CITV have started to take over the usual voice-over spots.

**ToonSleuth:**
Yes! I noticed that too. It seems like Pop is really integrating the best of both worlds, bringing in those memorable CITV announcers to add a nostalgic flair to their presentation. As for the lineup, I’m beyond thrilled. They’ve actually listened to our wishes. There’s a perfect balance of classic revivals and new originals!

**DetailedDecoder:**
Here’s the breakdown of the full lineup for those who missed it:
- **MoonGoons Return**: No surprise there, but still super exciting!
- **Classic Comebacks**: Titles like "Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go!" are making a grand return. 
- **New Originals**: A series called "Galactic Adventures" - sounds like a space-themed hit in the making.
- **Interactive Shows**: "Choose Your Adventure Live", where viewers vote on the outcome in real-time.

**AnimationAficionado:**
"Choose Your Adventure Live" is exactly the kind of innovative content I was hoping for! It seems like Pop is really setting the bar high for interactive entertainment. And bringing back SRMTHFG? That’s a massive win for us nostalgia fans.

**NostalgicNerd:**
Absolutely buzzing about SRMTHFG’s return! And the choice to bring in CITV announcers is a masterstroke. Their voices have been part of many of our childhoods. It bridges the gap between generations of viewers and adds a comforting, familiar feel to the channel.

**PixelPundit:**
What caught my eye is the commitment to original content. "Galactic Adventures" could be the fresh flagship show Pop needs. It’s refreshing to see new ideas being championed alongside the classics we love.

**SuperToonFan:**
Did anyone catch when these shows are scheduled to start airing? "Choose Your Adventure Live" sounds like it'll be a game-changer for interactive TV. Kudos to Pop for this bold programming shake-up. It’s like the perfect blend of past, present, and future.

**CosmicToonFan:**
The rollout for these new shows starts this spring, with "MoonGoons Return" leading the charge. The rest of the schedule will be staggered throughout the year to keep us hooked. Pop’s not just creating a lineup; they’re crafting an experience. Also, kudos for integrating our beloved CITV announcers. It’s like hearing from old friends.

**AdminUpdate:**
This new year is shaping up to be a groundbreaking era for Pop, thanks to feedback from fans like you! We’re excited to see how these changes enhance your viewing experience. Keep your eyes peeled for exclusive insider peeks and opportunities to engage with your favorite shows right here on the forum.

**End of Thread**

This community discussion encapsulates the enthusiasm and anticipation surrounding Pop’s ambitious new direction. The blend of nostalgia, innovation, and fan feedback illustrates a bright year ahead for the channel and its dedicated audience.
Write a follow up where a user asks if GoGoRiki is returning

**Thread Title:** Any Chance for a GoGoRiki Comeback on Pop’s New Lineup?

**RetroToonRanger:**
Hey everyone! With all the excitement around Pop’s new lineup and the return of some classic shows, I was wondering—has anyone heard anything about GoGoRiki (also known as Kikoriki) making a comeback? It was such a unique and entertaining show with a lot of heart. Would love to see it return along with everything else Pop is offering!

**CartoonConnoisseur:**
@RetroToonRanger Funny you should mention GoGoRiki! There hasn’t been any official word from Pop about it returning as part of the new lineup. However, considering the diverse range of shows they're bringing back and introducing, I wouldn’t rule it out entirely. Maybe it’s on their radar for future plans?

**ToonSleuth:**
So far, I haven’t seen GoGoRiki on any of the announced schedules or teaser previews. But, given the trend towards nostalgic shows, it's definitely within the realm of possibility. It might be worth reaching out to Pop directly or maybe starting a fan petition to show there's interest!

**AnimationAficionado:**
I loved GoGoRiki! Its humor was something else, and the educational aspects were woven in so cleverly. It's a show that definitely deserves a second run given the current programming direction. Maybe we should indeed start a social media campaign or something to bring it to Pop's attention?

**PixelPundit:**
That’s a brilliant idea, @AnimationAficionado. Shows like GoGoRiki have a timeless appeal, and with all the interactive and innovative content Pop is planning, it could fit in perfectly. I say we get a #BringBackGoGoRiki hashtag going and see if we can get Pop's attention.

**NostalgicNerd:**
Count me in on that hashtag campaign! #BringBackGoGoRiki could show Pop there’s a genuine appeal for the show's return. These kinds of campaigns have worked before for other series, so it’s definitely worth a shot!

**DigitalDreamer:**
Just dropped a tweet with the #BringBackGoGoRiki hashtag! If anyone here has fun memories or thoughts about why GoGoRiki should return, share them online. Let’s make some noise and maybe Pop will take notice and consider bringing it back.

**SuperToonFan:**
I’m on board! GoGoRiki had such a unique charm, and it offered something different from the usual fare. It’s amazing thinking about how it could be updated or even reimagined for today’s audience. Let’s spread the word and hopefully, Pop will listen! 💪 #BringBackGoGoRiki

**AdminNote:**
It’s wonderful to see such passion from our community! While we can’t make programming decisions here on the forum, we definitely encourage you to share your thoughts and who knows? Your enthusiasm might just bring GoGoRiki back into the conversation for future programming considerations. Keep the ideas and hashtags coming! 🌟

**End of Thread**

This animated discussion among fans showcases a vibrant community eager to see the return of their beloved show, GoGoRiki. With nostalgia driving many programming decisions, the mobilization of a fan-led campaign could potentially sway network execs to consider its revival amidst Pop’s evolving lineup.
Write a tweet with the full list of programming for 2024, no news on GoGoRiki yet

🌟 Exciting News Alert, #PopTVFans! 🌈 Here's the FULL programming lineup for 2024 📅🎉 Dive into a year of unstoppable adventures, laughter, and magical moments! Note: Still no news on #GoGoRiki, but keep the love coming! ❤️✨ #PopTV2024 #NewYearNewToons

1. 🚀 **MoonGoons Return** - The beloved adventures continue!
2. 🐵 **Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go!** - Classic heroics make a comeback.
3. 🌌 **Galactic Adventures** - Setting course for new horizons in our original epic!
4. 🔄 **Choose Your Adventure Live** - Where YOUR choices shape the story.
5. 🧩 **Mystery Mansion** - Solve puzzles and mysteries in this animated thriller.
6. 🎤 **PopTunes** - The latest hits meet animated antics.
7. 🐉 **Legends of the Ancients** - Uncover ancient secrets in our new mystical series.
8. 🎮 **Game On!** - Gamers and toons collide in this animated game-show extravaganza.
9. 🔍 **Detective Duo** - Follow the clues with our most adorable investigators.
10. 🌳 **EcoHeroes** - Saving the planet, one adventure at a time.

🎈More surprises to come! Keep your eyes peeled and your remotes ready! 🎈#StayTuned #AdventureAwaits #PopTVMagic
Write a tweet saying Game On is basically just Skatoony, And also here's the shows staying from the current lineup

🕹️ Big reveal time! 🎉 "Game On!" is hitting your screens in 2024 and guess what? It's basically our new take on the legendary #Skatoony - where gaming meets zany animation in a showdown like no other! 🏆✨ Plus, here's a sneak peek at the faves sticking around from our current lineup:

- 🌈 **Alvin & the Chipmunks** - Keeping the music and mischief going!
- 🦸‍♀️ **Miraculous Ladybug** - More miraculous adventures await.
- ⚡ **Pokemon** - The journey in the Pokémon world continues.
- 🍫 **Squish** - Stay squishy with more blobby fun!

Gear up for an epic year ahead with all the classics and fresh hits! 🎈 #GameOn2024 #ClassicAndNew #PopTVLineup
Write a follow up tweet confirming Gogoriki's fate

📢 Exciting Update for all #GoGoRiki Fans! 🎉 After much anticipation and listening to YOUR voices, we’re thrilled to announce that #GoGoRiki will be making a grand return to our screens in 2024! 🐷🐰🌟 Get ready for more wacky adventures and heartfelt stories from our beloved crew. Thank you for your passion and patience! Stay tuned for more updates. #GoGoRikiReturns #PopTV 🎈📺
